channel.title: Sam C Barth — RevOps, AI & CRM Strategy
channel.description: RevOps, AI, and CRM strategy from Sam C Barth.

item.title: Oracle's AI data center boom is about to meet real-world pushback
item.description: Oracle has clearly benefited from the AI infrastructure trade, and I understand why investors have been willing to reward that story. The market loves a clean narrative, and right now the narrative is simple: build data centers, support the AI buildout, and let the demand follow.

item.title: The most valuable AI use case isn't a product. It's a personal tool.
item.description: There is a version of AI that gets a lot of attention and a version that does not. The one that gets the attention is commercial: AI copilots, AI add-ons, AI agents that companies can license. The one that does not is quieter and, honestly, more genuinely useful. It is people bui

item.title: The Bricks & Minifigs Lego mess is a resale-business problem waiting to happen
item.description: There's a whole controversy going on right now with Bricks & Minifigs, the Lego resale chain, and one of their stores allegedly walking off with a man's father's lifetime collection of Star Wars Legos. The way I see it, this is one of the huge parts of the resale business, especi

item.title: Costco's gas boom is a lesson in leveraging a great core business
item.description: Costco is a super cool type of business model, being able to have the wholesale prices for people, and as long as they buy enough of it and get enough savings, then it covers the membership fees. So in order to get the quote unquote savings, they have to spend a lot. It's a prett

item.title: Dell's best day ever tells you where enterprise AI is actually going
item.description: Dell's stock had its best single day ever on Thursday — up 32%. The reason: AI server revenue. Not AI features. Not AI announcements. Not a partnership press release. Actual infrastructure revenue from enterprises buying servers to run AI workloads at scale. That's a materially d

item.title: Samsung just promised $400K bonuses. That's an AI story too.
item.description: Samsung Electronics just approved a profit-sharing deal that could pay semiconductor workers average bonuses of nearly $400,000. The agreement runs ten years and allocates 10.5% of the semiconductor division's operating profit directly to employees. That's a significant number. A

item.title: AI is making cybersecurity harder — and that's a GTM problem too
item.description: An award-winning ethical hacker made the rounds this week with a warning worth paying attention to: AI cybersecurity tools are approaching the point where they will outpace human researchers at finding vulnerabilities. AI systems are already accelerating how fast security flaws g

item.title: Where AI actually falls apart
item.description: I've been diving into Claude, Claude Co-Work, and Claude Dispatch this weekend, really trying to see how much of an agentic system process I can build to lessen my workload and focus on what I actually need to do. Today especially, I started to see what people mean when they say

item.title: The shift from "AI tools" to AI with actual business context
item.description: One thing I'm watching closely in the HubSpot ecosystem right now is the shift from "AI tools" to AI with actual business context. HubSpot's recent Spring Spotlight updates around AEO, AI agents, and contextual CRM data feel less like simple AI features and more like the beginnin

item.title: HubSpot vs. Salesforce: two very different bets on AI agents
item.description: Salesforce has been loud about what AI means for their workforce. Marc Benioff said they would not hire new engineers this year because AI is doing the work. They've since cut 4,000 customer service roles. The message is pretty clear: agents in, people out. HubSpot is taking a di

item.title: HubSpot's AI push is a signal every RevOps team should read
item.description: HubSpot continuing to invest heavily into AI-powered automation is a reminder that CRM platforms are turning into operational systems, not just databases of record. For RevOps teams, this matters. The tools are getting smarter, but the strategy still has to come from people who u

item.title: Site is live, built and deployed with AI
item.description: I built this entire site using Claude Code, Anthropic's AI coding tool. Start to finish: under an hour. GitHub repo, GitHub Pages deployment, custom workflow, all of it. The workflow is simple: describe what you want, review the output, push. No boilerplate hunting, no getting lo

item.title: What 10 years in marketing ops actually teaches you
item.description: I've spent over 10 years helping organizations market themselves online: generating leads, tracking attribution, managing CRMs, and turning raw data into reporting that actually drives decisions. The answer is always the same: garbage in, garbage out. The best automation in the w
